free_text: Charged particle activation of medium z elements II.
Proton excitation functions
Description of the experimental set-up
Range table
Thin high purity metal foils for Cu and Pd (25 mg/cm2)
and vacuum-vapour deposits (1 mg/cm2) of Mo, Cd, Sn,
Te and Cs on thin Al backings (3.4 mg/cm2)
20 MeV primary proton energy was used for irradiation.
Each point is the average of at least two stack foil
experiments.
Proton energies were calculated at the centre of each
foils using Williamson's table
The energy calculated for each foils are accurate
The overall error in the cross section determination
is estimated at <20%, including errors due to
evaporation losses during irradiation.
Weighing error, foil thickness and counting geometry
errors were determined to be within 5%.
Table 3 of the J.Radioanal.Chem.31(1976)447
